mnemonics: Inside Out: Interior points are inside with space around (open ball).
Open for Business: Open sets have all points as interior points.
keyPoints: The interior of a set is the set of all interior points.
The interior is always an open set.
The interior of A is the largest open set contained in A.
commonMistakes: Confusing interior with closure (interior vs. boundary).
Assuming every point in a set is an interior point (only true for open sets).
Forgetting that interior depends on the metric space.
